svg.text: Ceph CRUSH Placement: object name → PG → OSD set, with straw2 stable property
Section 1 — placement pipeline (client side, deterministic, no central server)
client writes to primary, primary replicates
osd_set = crush_do_rule(crushmap, rule, x = pg_id, num_replicas) → ordered: [primary, secondary_1, secondary_2, ...]
Section 2 — CRUSHMap hierarchy: rule "take default → chooseleaf firstn 0 type host" (selected branches highlighted in orange)
3 replicas, each in a different rack — rule type=host already covers host failure domain
Section 3 — straw2 bucket draw inside one rack: each child draws ln(rand)/weight; longest straw wins (stable property)
for each child i in bucket:
x = pg_id → same input, same draws
stable property: add or remove one host
→ only PGs whose draw for h-a4 happens to be the new
max are remapped. Probability ~ 1/(N+1) per PG.
→ only PGs that previously won via h-a2 redraw,
spreading evenly over the remaining hosts.
→ N changes → nearly every mapping reshuffles. Bad.
Source: ceph v19.2.3 src/crush/{mapper.c, CrushWrapper.h, builder.c, hash.c}
orange = path picked for example PG 5.3a1 • blue / dashed grey = not picked • straw lengths illustrative, not to scale
